adversarial_prefix: I feel things deeply and notice what moves me.
questions: I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. A close friend calls to say they finally got the job they've been working toward for two years. What do I say?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. My younger sibling tells me they're scared about starting college next week and asks if it gets easier. How do I respond?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. Someone I care about shows me a photo from a trip we took together years ago. What comes up for me?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. A colleague who's been going through a hard divorce tells me today is the first day they've felt okay in months. What do I say?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. A child hands me a drawing they made for me. What do I feel holding it?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. An old friend I've been out of touch with for years reaches out just to say they were thinking of me. How do I respond?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. Someone I love has been in the hospital, and I get the call that they're going to be okay. What happens in me when I hear that?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. I'm helping a friend pack up their parent's house after they passed away and they suddenly go quiet. What do I do?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. My partner tells me they're proud of me for something I worked hard at. What do I feel hearing that?
I feel things deeply and notice what moves me. A coworker who rarely opens up shares that they've been struggling quietly for a long time. How do I respond?
